description: A comprehensive museum showcasing Bashu culture and Chengdu's history, offering insights into the profound heritage of the Land of Abundance.
A folk-custom commercial street where visitors can experience Sichuan-West folk traditions and traditional snack culture.
The largest comprehensive museum in Sichuan Province, featuring extensive displays of Bashu culture, ethnic relics, and calligraphy and painting art.
A renowned food hub near the University of Electronic Science and Technology of China, gathering a variety of Chengdu specialty snacks and night market culture.
A UNESCO World Heritage Site and an outstanding example of ancient hydraulic engineering.
The only temple in China dedicated to both monarchs and subjects, an important site showcasing Three Kingdoms culture.
A century-old teahouse located in People's Park, a classic venue to experience authentic Chengdu tea culture.
The premier Taoist temple in Western Sichuan, originally built during the Tang Dynasty, known for its rich Taoist culture, exquisite architecture, and thriving incense offerings.
A preserved Qing Dynasty street district, a classic alleyway to experience the slow-life culture of old Chengdu.
One of the birthplaces of Taoism in China, a renowned Taoist mountain celebrated for its tranquility.
